criteria_checked.note: B1 RESOLVED. Rev 2 (E1 lines 11-18; Proposed Changes 135-149) now explicitly states the `start` subcommand is SPLIT OUT of the shared `exec python3 "$RCORE" "$sub" "$@"` group (confirmed at bin/dmc:374-376) into a NON-exec captured call (`python3 "$RCORE" start <argv>; rc=$?`) with both streams flowing through UNTOUCHED, while every other run subcommand keeps `exec` verbatim. 'Captured' unambiguously means rc-captured (non-exec), NOT stdout-captured — the plan says streams flow through untouched, so no double-buffer/reprint. Implementable and non-ambiguous.

r1 gap closed. Back-compat AC (lines 178-187) now pins BOTH paths: SUCCESS = stdout+exit identical to direct RCORE plus exactly one stderr WARNING; REFUSE = exit code AND both streams byte-identical to direct RCORE (bin/dmc adds nothing on non-zero rc). E2 (33-40) makes the WARNING success-path-only (RCORE exit 0). Verified against tests/fixtures/m6/test-adversarial.sh case_verdictfloor `out=$(... 2>&1)` refuse capture — the plan's byte-identity posture is stronger than that grep-based test requires, i.e. conservatively correct.
Defect root-cause, 4x-compensation history, teardown precedent, and envelope all accurate and in-tree-verifiable. Unchanged from r1 (was met).

r1 gap closed. Plan adds `SCOPELOCKLIB="$HERE/lib/dmc-scope-lock.py"` (absolute) — matches the existing house pattern (RCORE bin/dmc:11, SKILLSMIRRORLIB bin/dmc:24); no cwd-relative bin/lib path. Mirror `.agents/skills/dmc-start-work/SKILL.md` CONFIRMED to exist at exactly 2,577 B as the plan states. Verified `--out <root>/.harness/runs/<id>/scope.lock.json` equals default_lock_path(root,run_id) (dmc-scope-lock.py:337-338), so is_default_dest (line 354-356, os.path.abspath both sides, same root string) returns True ⇒ operative snapshot IS recorded (line 515) ⇒ the run is armed-for-real and the bash-radius deny probe (AC-1) is valid.

B2 RESOLVED and the CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR false-fallback is removed from Assumptions #1 (line 215 now states neither CLI reads it). --root threading verified correct: runs_dir(root)=<root>/.harness/runs (run-lifecycle:229), pointer read <root>/.harness/runs/current-run-id (pointer_path:236-237), compile consumes --root (scope-lock:931 root=os.path.abspath(a.root)). ADJUDICATION of the `--validate ... --root` question: scope-lock's --validate branch (dmc-scope-lock.py:901-912) NEVER references a.root; --root is a global argparse arg (line 890) so `--validate LOCK --root DIR` PARSES cleanly and is IGNORED — HARMLESS REDUNDANCY, NOT a misbuild (argparse does not reject it). The plan's 'compile+validate both invoked with --root' is therefore safe (redundant on validate). ADVISORY: the Risks table line 207 mitigation still reads 'fixture uses --root/CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR isolation' — a stale residual reference contradicting the corrected Assumptions #1 and the E4 body (156-158, 'the CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR idea was WRONG'); cosmetic, non-load-bearing since the operative instruction is --root everywhere; recommend dropping '/CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR' from that cell.

Tight. Relevant Files is now 5 editable rows incl. the mirror as REQUIRED lockstep (resolving the r1 conditional). Core modules (dmc-run-lifecycle.py, dmc-scope-lock.py, hooks, schemas, frozen dmc-v* tools, installer) byte-untouched and in Out-of-Scope. --scope-input kept optional (back-compat). bin/dmc==enforcement-class (FLAG expected, no G4 override since .claude/skills is not DEFAULT_PROTECTED) — consistent with v1.1/v1.1.2 precedent.

Security lens (mandatory, enforcement-class bin/dmc). Fail-closed teardown ordering CORRECT: cmd_suspend→_resolve→read_pointer (run-lifecycle:419-433) resolves the run via the pointer, so `run suspend` MUST run BEFORE pointer removal — the plan has exactly this order (lines 28-31, 146-148). Teardown fires on compile OR validate failure ⇒ REFUSED-ARMING + suspend + pointer rm + exit 3, killing the false-armed state. After a rc==0 start the run is RUNNING so RUNNING→SUSPENDED is a valid transition. Residual low risk (suspend itself failing mid-teardown leaves RUNNING+no-lock) unchanged from r1, rated low. Net security-positive: closes the L1-stands-down false-armed gap. scope-input is compiler-validated JSON; no secret/env reads; no injection given house-style quoting.

LOCAL-commit ceiling on claude/dmc-fable-core, staged-set==in-scope, clean-tree --all 802/3/3 with .codex/config.toml stashed (V15 gotcha handled). The deterministic teardown IS the in-flight rollback for a half-armed run. Push/CI/main a separate human gate.
